recipes.title: Cinnamon Roll Overnight Oats (Blended)
recipes.description: These blended cinnamon roll overnight oats are creamy, refined sugar free, and ready with just 5 minutes of prep. The perfect healthy meal prep breakfast.

image.alt: Blended cinnamon roll overnight oats in a jar topped with white chocolate

instructions.text: Add all of the ingredients to a high power blender.
Blend until the oats are completely smooth.
Add in a few extra tablespoons of milk if you prefer a runnier texture and blend again.
Transfer to an airtight container and place in the fridge overnight.
Enjoy!

notes: Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days.
If they thicken overnight, stir in a splash of milk before serving.
Add protein powder and blend for a high protein version — extra milk may be needed.

seo.metaDescription: These blended cinnamon roll overnight oats are creamy, refined sugar free, and ready with just 5 minutes of prep. The perfect healthy meal prep breakfast.

pinterest.description: Blended cinnamon roll overnight oats — creamy, refined sugar free and ready in 5 minutes of prep! The perfect healthy meal prep breakfast 🤎
pinterest.title: Cinnamon Roll Overnight Oats (Blended)

instagram.caption: if you hate the texture of regular overnight oats, try blending them 🤎 cinnamon roll overnight oats that taste like dessert for breakfast — 5 min prep, meal prep friendly! recipe on the blog!

story.subtitle: smooth, dreamy 5-minute meal prep breakfast
paragraphs: If you love the cozy flavours of cinnamon rolls but want something nourishing enough to eat for breakfast every day, these blended cinnamon roll overnight oats are exactly what you need.
They have all the warm cinnamon sweetness you crave, but in a healthy, refined sugar free form that's perfect for meal prep.
And if you've ever struggled with the texture of traditional overnight oats, blending them completely changes the game.
It takes just 5 minutes to prep, sets overnight in the fridge, and makes breakfast effortless.

faq.question: How do I store cinnamon roll overnight oats?
faq.answer: Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days. If they thicken overnight, simply stir in a splash of milk before serving.

faq.question: What type of oats should I use?
faq.answer: Rolled oats or porridge oats work best. Steel cut oats are not ideal since they can remain too firm.

faq.question: Are cinnamon roll overnight oats gluten free?
faq.answer: Yes, if you use certified gluten-free oats. Oats are naturally gluten-free, but cross-contamination can occur during processing.

faq.question: Can I make high protein overnight oats?
faq.answer: Yes — add your favourite protein powder and blend. You will likely need to add more milk depending on absorbency.

faq.question: Why are these overnight oats blended?
faq.answer: Blending creates a smoother, creamier pudding-like texture instead of the typical thick or chewy oat consistency.

faq.question: What topping is used?
faq.answer: I used white chocolate for subtle sweetness. Yogurt of your choice would also work great.

recipes.title: 4 Ingredient Matcha Marshmallows
recipes.description: These healthy 4-ingredient homemade matcha marshmallows are soft, fluffy, and refined sugar free. Easy to make and perfect for a better for you sweet treat.

image.alt: Soft fluffy homemade matcha marshmallows dusted with matcha powder on a white surface

instructions.text: In your stand mixer add the gelatin and 1/2 cup of water and let it bloom.
To a saucepan add the matcha, 1/2 cup of water and maple syrup. Let it heat for 5 minutes on medium heat but don't let it boil.
Turn the stand mixer on medium speed and slowly pour in the hot liquid.
Turn to high speed for 6–8 minutes until the texture begins to fluff and thicken.
Quickly pour into a lined 8 inch / 20cm square baking tray — work fast so it doesn't set in the bowl\!
Refrigerate for at least 5 hours or until set.
Slice and enjoy\!\!

notes: Work quickly when pouring into the tray — the mixture sets fast.
For optimal results, let set overnight in the fridge.
Ensure the maple syrup gets hot enough so the gelatin dissolves properly.

seo.metaTitle: 4 Ingredient Matcha Marshmallows (Refined Sugar Free) | Yourwellnessgirly
seo.metaDescription: These healthy 4-ingredient homemade matcha marshmallows are soft, fluffy, and refined sugar free. Easy to make and perfect for a better for you sweet treat.

pinterest.description: These 4-ingredient matcha marshmallows are soft, fluffy, and refined sugar free\! So easy to make at home 🍵✨
pinterest.title: 4 Ingredient Matcha Marshmallows (Refined Sugar Free)

instagram.caption: once you make homemade marshmallows you can't go back 🍵 4 ingredients, refined sugar free, soft and fluffy — these matcha marshmallows are everything\! recipe on the blog\!

story.subtitle: soft, fluffy refined sugar free homemade treat
paragraphs: if you've ever wanted to make homemade marshmallows but thought it sounded complicated, this recipe will change your mind.
they're soft, fluffy, lightly sweet, and completely different from store-bought versions loaded with additives.

Plus these matcha marshmallows are made with healthier gut soothing ingredients — gelatin is a source of collagen and contains amino acids that help support gut lining integrity and overall digestive health.

faq.question: Are these homemade marshmallows refined sugar free?
faq.answer: Yes, these matcha marshmallows are made with maple syrup meaning they are refined sugar free and have a lower glycemic index compared to traditional marshmallows.

faq.question: Why are my homemade marshmallows not fluffy?
faq.answer: Marshmallows need to be whipped long enough to properly incorporate air. It's best to go by texture: the mixture should become thick, glossy, and significantly increased in volume before pouring into your pan.

tips: Once the marshmallows have reached whip texture, pour them quickly into the baking tray to avoid it setting in the bowl\!
Let the marshmallows set overnight in the fridge for optimal results.
Ensure that the maple syrup gets hot enough so the gelatin dissolves properly.

recipes.title: Healthy Sugar-Free Apple Fritters (Gluten-Free & Naturally Sweetened)
recipes.description: My viral healthy added sugar-free apple fritters are gluten-free, naturally sweetened and baked, not fried — the perfect sweet snack.

image.alt: Cross section of a sugar free apple fritter showing tender interior with visible apple pieces and golden exterior

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 190°C/180°C fan.
In a bowl, crack the egg and add the melted coconut oil. Whisk until combined.
Dice the apples very finely (approx half an inch thick) and add to the bowl.
Mix in the cinnamon and add oats, rice flour and baking powder, mixing until combined.
Line a baking tray. Scoop a heaped tablespoon of the batter for each fritter.
Bake for 14-17 minutes or until slightly golden brown.

notes: This is a small-batch recipe — double the ingredients for a larger serving.
Dice the apples very finely to help the batter bind properly and cook evenly.
Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 24 hours, or refrigerate for 3-4 days.
Optional topping: drizzle with maple syrup, icing sugar, coconut butter, or powdered monk fruit.

seo.metaTitle: Healthy Sugar-Free Apple Fritters (Gluten-Free & Naturally Sweetened) | Yourwellnessgirly
seo.metaDescription: My viral healthy added sugar-free apple fritters are gluten-free, naturally sweetened and baked, not fried — the perfect sweet snack.

pinterest.description: My viral healthy added sugar-free apple fritters! Gluten-free, baked not fried, and naturally sweetened 🍎✨
pinterest.title: Healthy Sugar-Free Apple Fritters (Gluten-Free & Naturally Sweetened)

instagram.caption: Over 20 MILLION people have seen these healthy apple fritters 🍎 They're added sugar-free, gluten-free, baked not fried — and SO good! Recipe on the blog!

story.subtitle: the viral apple fritters everyone's obsessed with
paragraphs: fun fact: i was going to gatekeep these added sugar-free and gluten-free apple fritters… but not for the reason you might think. after developing this recipe, i made them three times in one week (yuppp) and found myself constantly craving them. i shared this one anyway and the response was positively overwhelming with over 20 MILLION people seeing them!

these apple fritters are now one of my most viral recipes of all time. they're a healthier twist on traditional apple fritters: baked instead of fried, made with better-for-you ingredients, and naturally sweetened. They make for a great healthy dessert, breakfast on the go, or sweet snack.

faq.question: How do I store sugar-free apple fritters?
faq.answer: Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 24 hours. Refrigerate for 3-4 days. Reheat in the oven or air fryer to restore texture.

faq.question: Are these apple fritters completely sugar-free?
faq.answer: No — they contain naturally occurring sugars from apples. However, the recipe is added sugar-free and refined sugar-free.

faq.question: What can I substitute for rice flour?
faq.answer: All-purpose flour works well, or a 1:1 gluten-free flour blend to keep the recipe gluten-free.

faq.question: Why does this recipe have very little batter?
faq.answer: The batter is designed to be apple-forward, lightly coating and binding the apples rather than surrounding them like a traditional deep-fried fritter.

faq.question: Why are my apple fritters dry?
faq.answer: Double-check that you weighed ingredients in grams and that apples are diced finely. To fix dry batter, add an extra egg or a splash of milk.

tips: This is a small-batch apple fritter recipe. Double the ingredients if you'd like a larger serving.
Dice the apples very finely to help the batter bind properly and cook evenly.
Use naturally sweet, juicy apples — fuji, gala, honeycrisp, or pink lady all work great.

recipes.title: Healthy Sugar-Free Blueberry Fritters
recipes.description: These healthy sugar-free blueberry fritters are baked, soft like blueberry muffin tops crossed with sweet bread. No added sugar, ready in 20 minutes and made with healthier ingredients.

image.alt: Healthy sugar-free blueberry fritters on a baking tray, golden on the edges with juicy blueberries

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 170°C/160°C fan.
In a small bowl, crack the eggs, add the melted coconut oil and vanilla. Whisk until combined.
Add in the oats, flour and baking powder and fold until just combined.
Pour in the frozen blueberries and fold until evenly distributed through the batter. It should be almost equal amounts blueberries to batter.
Spoon onto a lined baking tray.
Bake for 15 to 20 minutes until the edges are golden brown.
Optional: drizzle with maple syrup, powdered sweetener, coconut butter or icing sugar for added sweetness. Enjoy!

notes: This is a small-batch recipe making 6 fritters. Feel free to double for a larger batch.
If your blueberries aren't very sweet, add a drizzle of maple syrup or your favourite sugar-free sweetener on top.
Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 24 hours or refrigerate for up to 3 days.
Reheat in the oven or air fryer for a few minutes to restore the golden edges.

seo.metaTitle: Healthy Sugar-Free Blueberry Fritters (Baked & Naturally Sweetened) | Yourwellnessgirly
seo.metaDescription: These healthy sugar-free blueberry fritters are baked, soft like blueberry muffin tops crossed with sweet bread. No added sugar, ready in 20 minutes and made with healthier ingredients.

pinterest.description: Healthy sugar-free blueberry fritters! 🫐 Baked, naturally sweetened and ready in 20 minutes. Like a blueberry muffin top meets sweet bread!
pinterest.title: Healthy Sugar-Free Blueberry Fritters (Baked & Naturally Sweetened)

instagram.caption: if you loved my viral apple fritters, you NEED to try these blueberry fritters 🫐✨ baked, no added sugar and ready in 20 minutes! recipe on the blog ➡️

story.subtitle: why you'll love them
paragraphs: if you love blueberry muffins but want something a little lighter and quicker, these healthy blueberry fritters are going to be your new obsession. the texture is somewhere between a blueberry muffin top and soft sweet bread: golden on the edges, fluffy in the centre, and packed with juicy blueberries in every bite. they're baked instead of fried and naturally sweetened, with the option to add your favourite sugar-free sweetener if you prefer.

this small-batch recipe makes approximately 6 fritters, perfect for when you want something sweet that you can adjust to your desired sweetness. they're a great healthy sweet snack, quick meal prep breakfast, or on-the-go treat. if you're one of the millions who tried and loved my viral sugar-free, gluten-free apple fritters, this blueberry version should be your next bake. let me know — are you team apple fritters or team blueberry fritters?

tips: Feel free to halve the recipe if you are baking for one!
Opt for sweeter blueberries for the best taste.
Work quickly to scoop the fritters once you have added the frozen blueberries.

recipes.title: Healthy Chocolate Banana Bites
recipes.description: Easy 15-minute healthy chocolate banana bites with only 5 ingredients. No-bake, vegan, and gluten-free dessert perfect for satisfying your sweet cravings guilt-free!

instructions.text: In a medium bowl, mash the ripe bananas until smooth.
Add almond butter and mix until well combined.
Stir in cocoa powder and maple syrup until the mixture is smooth and chocolatey.
Fold in the mini chocolate chips gently.
Using a small cookie scoop or spoon, form mixture into 12 bite-sized balls and place on a parchment-lined tray.
Refrigerate for 10-15 minutes to firm up. Serve chilled and enjoy!

notes: Store in the refrigerator for up to 5 days in an airtight container.
For a nut-free version, substitute sunflower seed butter for almond butter.
You can roll the bites in shredded coconut or chopped nuts for extra texture.
These freeze well for up to 3 months - just thaw for 5 minutes before serving.

seo.metaTitle: Healthy Chocolate Banana Bites Recipe | 15-Minute No-Bake Dessert | Yourwellnessgirly
seo.metaDescription: Easy 15-minute healthy chocolate banana bites with only 5 ingredients. No-bake, vegan, and gluten-free dessert perfect for satisfying your sweet cravings guilt-free!

pinterest.description: Easy 15-minute healthy chocolate banana bites! No-bake, vegan & gluten-free with only 5 ingredients. Perfect guilt-free dessert! 🍌🍫
pinterest.title: Healthy Chocolate Banana Bites - No-Bake Vegan Dessert

instagram.caption: Who else is obsessed with these 15-minute chocolate banana bites? 🍌🍫 Only 5 ingredients and SO good! Recipe on the blog ➡️

story.subtitle: why you'll love them
paragraphs: These chocolate banana bites are the perfect guilt-free treat when you're craving something sweet! Made with just 5 wholesome ingredients, they're naturally sweetened with ripe bananas and maple syrup. The combination of creamy almond butter, rich cocoa powder, and mini chocolate chips creates the most satisfying bite that actually nourishes your body.

I love making a batch of these on Sunday for easy grab-and-go snacks throughout the week. They're perfect for post-workout fuel, afternoon energy boosts, or when you need something sweet that won't leave you crashing later. Plus, they're ready in just 15 minutes!

faq.question: Can I make these nut-free?
faq.answer: Yes! Simply substitute the almond butter with sunflower seed butter or tahini for a nut-free version that's just as delicious.

faq.question: How should I store these bites?
faq.answer: Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days. They actually taste even better after chilling!

faq.question: Can I freeze these for later?
faq.answer: Absolutely! These freeze beautifully for up to 3 months. Just thaw for 5-10 minutes before enjoying.

faq.question: My mixture seems too wet, what should I do?
faq.answer: If your bananas are very ripe and the mixture is too wet, add a tablespoon of oat flour or coconut flour to help bind everything together.

faq.question: Can I roll these in anything for extra flavor?
faq.answer: Yes! Try rolling them in shredded coconut, chopped nuts, or even more cocoa powder for extra indulgence.

tips: Use very ripe bananas for the best natural sweetness and easiest mashing
Chill the mixture for 10 minutes if it's too soft to shape
A small cookie scoop makes perfectly uniform bites every time
These taste even better the next day after the flavors have melded

recipes.title: No-Bake Mango Yogurt Chocolate Bites
recipes.description: Refreshing 3-ingredient summer dessert! Creamy Greek yogurt and fresh mango wrapped in rich dark chocolate - naturally sweetened and ready in 10 minutes prep.

image.alt: No-Bake Mango Yogurt Chocolate Bites - healthy dessert recipe

instructions.text: Melt the dark chocolate using a double boiler or microwave in 30-second intervals. Pour melted chocolate into your silicone molds, covering the bottom and the sides. Flash freeze for 10 minutes or until set.
Dice the mango into approx. inch thick pieces. In a medium bowl, combine the Greek yogurt and diced mango. Gently fold together until evenly mixed.
Remove the molds from the freezer. Add 2-3 teaspoons of the mango yogurt mixture to each mold on top of the set chocolate base.
Drizzle or spread the remaining melted chocolate over the yogurt layer to create the top.
Freeze for 2 hours or until completely set. Remove from molds and sprinkle with sea salt. Enjoyyy

notes: For vegan version, substitute Greek yogurt with coconut yogurt - it pairs beautifully with mango!
Store in refrigerator for up to 3 days or freezer for up to 1 week
Silicone molds work best for easy removal. Ice cube trays work too!
Can use frozen mango - thaw completely and drain excess liquid first
Let sit 1-2 minutes at room temperature before serving for easier eating
equipment: Silicone molds or ice cube trays

seo.metaTitle: No-Bake Mango Yogurt Chocolate Bites | 3 Ingredient Summer Dessert | Yourwellnessgirly
seo.metaDescription: Easy 3-ingredient no-bake mango yogurt chocolate bites! Refreshing summer dessert with Greek yogurt, fresh mango, and dark chocolate. Gluten-free, refined sugar-free, ready in 10 minutes prep!

pinterest.description: Easy 3-ingredient no-bake mango yogurt chocolate bites! Perfect refreshing summer dessert 🥭🍫 Gluten-free & naturally sweetened!
pinterest.title: No-Bake Mango Yogurt Chocolate Bites - 3 Ingredient Summer Dessert

instagram.caption: This is your sign to make these healthy 3 ingredient mango yogurt clusters!! If you love mango and chocolate then these no bake cuties will be your new fave dessert 🥭🍫 Save this for the perfect summer dessert!

story.subtitle: why you'll love them
paragraphs: If you're looking for the perfect no-bake sweet treat that actually satisfies your sweet tooth, you have to try these no bake mango yogurt bites. The natural sweetness of mango paired with creamy Greek yogurt (sneaky protein + probiotics) and topped with dark chocolate is such a good combo. The richness of the 70% dark chocolate balances out the sweetness of the mango perfectly, making these one of my favourite no-bake healthy desserts.

I love these all year round, but they're especially refreshing to make in the summer when you don't want to turn the oven on. Highly recommend meal prepping a batch and keeping them in the freezer for when your sweet tooth hits (just let them thaw for about 10 minutes before!). The best part? They're made with just 3 simple ingredients that you probably already have on hand.

faq.question: Can I make these mango yogurt bites vegan?
faq.answer: Yes! Simply substitute the Greek yogurt with your favorite plant-based yogurt. Coconut yogurt works particularly well and complements the mango flavor beautifully.

faq.question: How long do these mango chocolate bites last?
faq.answer: Store in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or in the freezer for up to 1 week. For best texture, serve directly from the freezer.

faq.question: What type of mold should I use?
faq.answer: Silicone molds work best as they're easy to remove the bites from. You can also use ice cube trays or mini muffin tins lined with paper liners.

faq.question: Can I use frozen mango instead of fresh?
faq.answer: Yes! Thaw frozen mango completely and drain excess liquid before mixing with yogurt for the best texture.

faq.question: What chocolate should I use?
faq.answer: Use high-quality dark chocolate with 70% cocoa content. For refined sugar-free option, choose stevia-sweetened dark chocolate.

faq.question: Can these be made ahead for meal prep?
faq.answer: Absolutely! These are perfect for meal prep. Make a batch and keep them in the freezer for up to a week. They make great grab-and-go summer treats.

tips: Let the bites sit at room temperature for 1-2 minutes before serving for easier eating
Use the ripest mango you can find for the best natural sweetness
If your chocolate is too thick, add a tiny bit of coconut oil to thin it out

alternatives: Regular yogurt (though texture will be less thick)

recipes.title: Healthy No Bake Brownie Batter Bars (4 Ingredients)
recipes.description: No bake healthy brownie batter bars made with just 4 ingredients. Gluten-free, refined sugar-free and ready in minutes.

image.alt: Hand holding no-bake dark chocolate brownie bars with glossy topping and sea salt flakes

instructions.text: Add all of the ingredients apart from the dark chocolate to a food processor and blend until combined.
Line a small container and transfer the batter. Press the batter down firmly.
Melt the dark chocolate and spread on top of the pressed batter.
Place in the freezer for 20 minutes or until the chocolate is set.
Remove from freezer, sprinkle with sea salt if desired, slice and enjoy!

recipes.title: 3 Ingredient Coconut Truffles
recipes.description: These healthy 3-ingredient coconut truffles are no-bake, simple to make, and packed with coconut flavour. The perfect treat for when you want something quick and wholesome.

image.alt: 3 ingredient coconut truffles rolled in desiccated coconut on a plate

instructions.text: In a bowl measure out the coconut butter and melt it.
Pour in the condensed coconut milk and mix with the coconut butter until combined.
Add in the desiccated coconut and mix.
Shape into balls — batter will be slightly sticky but malleable.
Roll each ball in extra desiccated coconut.
Place in the freezer for 15 minutes until they look set.
Enjoy!!

notes: Let each ball sit out at room temperature for a few minutes before enjoying.
Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freezer for up to 1 month.

seo.metaDescription: These healthy 3-ingredient coconut truffles are no-bake, simple to make, and packed with coconut flavour. The perfect treat for when you want something quick and wholesome.

pinterest.description: These healthy 3-ingredient coconut truffles are no-bake, dairy-free and packed with coconut flavour! Like Raffaello but better-for-you 🥥✨
pinterest.title: 3 Ingredient Coconut Truffles (No-Bake & Dairy-Free)

instagram.caption: 3 ingredients. no oven. pure coconut heaven 🥥 these no-bake coconut truffles are so easy and SO good — raffaello vibes but dairy free! recipe on the blog!

story.subtitle: why you'll love them
paragraphs: if you're a coconut lover, then you've just found your perfect match in dessert form. these 3-ingredient coconut truffles are exactly what you need. they're no-bake, easy to make, and packed with coconut goodness in every single bite.

they have the perfect truffle texture — firm on the outside, soft on the inside, with just enough crunch from the desiccated coconut. if you're a raffaello fan, you'll definitely love these too. they have a very similar vibe, but are dairy free and made with simpler, better-for-you ingredients.

with coconut in all three ingredients, they're packed with flavour. they're the ideal healthy no bake coconut treat for when you want something sweet without the hassle. you can store them in the freezer, which makes these coconut truffles perfect for meal prep as well.
this 3-ingredient coconut truffle recipe is truly foolproof and such a crowd pleaser. they make a delicious addition to any dinner table or party spread.

faq.question: How do I store 3-ingredient coconut truffles?
faq.answer: Store the coconut truffles in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. For longer storage, keep them in the freezer for up to 1 month. Allow frozen truffles to sit at room temperature for a few minutes before serving.

faq.question: Are these healthy coconut truffles completely sugar-free?
faq.answer: You can make these coconut truffles added sugar free by using a sugar free coconut condensed milk. Coconut has naturally occurring sugars which will always be present.

faq.question: Are coconut truffles gluten free?
faq.answer: Yes, these 3-ingredient coconut truffles are completely gluten free, dairy free and refined sugar free depending on the condensed coconut milk used.

faq.question: Do coconut truffles need to be refrigerated?
faq.answer: Yes, because these coconut truffles are made with coconut butter and condensed coconut milk, refrigeration helps them firm up and maintain their shape. They can sit at room temperature briefly when serving, but should be stored chilled for best texture.

tips: Let each ball sit out at room temperature for a few minutes before enjoying.
